Itemized  ·  Procedures  ·  Carpal tunnel release  ·  with Blue Cross Blue Shield

Carpal tunnel release with Blue Cross Blue Shield.

Negotiated rates Blue Cross Blue Shield pays for carpal tunnel release at 54 US hospitals. Range: $1 to $16,662. Your specific cost depends on your plan tier, deductible status, and coinsurance — see the comparison tool to model your exact out-of-pocket.

Estimate what you'd actually pay with Blue Cross Blue Shield.

Add your deductible status and coinsurance to the comparison tool. We'll estimate your out-of-pocket per hospital.

Open comparison →

About these rates.

Hospitals are required by federal law (45 CFR 180.50) to publish the rates they negotiated with each insurer for each procedure. These numbers are the rates the hospital published for Blue Cross Blue Shield plans, pulled directly from each hospital's machine-readable file.

The range column reflects different Blue Cross Blue Shield plan tiers (HMO, PPO, EPO, etc.). Your plan picks one number out of that range. Your actual out-of-pocket depends on your deductible status, copay, and coinsurance, which the comparison tool can model when you fill them in.

Cash-pay rates are often cheaper than the negotiated rate, especially for high-deductible plans. Worth comparing both — the Carpal tunnel release overview shows the cash-pay column alongside.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). CMS HCAHPS patient survey (dgck-syfz). Last refresh: 2026-04-27.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
